mnemonics: TESTING is for Separating Truth (T-Test, S-Split)
keyPoints: A test set is used to provide an unbiased evaluation of a final model fit on the training data.
The data should be split into training, validation, and test sets to prevent data leakage and overfitting.
The performance on the test set is considered the best estimate of the model's performance on new, unseen data.
commonMistakes: Training the model on the test data, which leads to overoptimistic performance estimates and data leakage.
Using the test set to make model selection or tuning decisions, effectively making it part of the training process.
Not having a hold-out test set at all, relying solely on cross-validation on the training data for final performance estimates.
